stories.title: Ukraine widens pressure on Russia's oil system with another deep strike on the Syzran refinery
stories.summary: Ukraine's long-range drone campaign is increasingly focused on Russia's refining network rather than symbolism alone. The new Syzran strike reinforces the idea that Kyiv is trying to squeeze fuel production, export revenue and military logistics at the same time.

links.headline: Ukraine says its drones hit another refinery deep inside Russia as long-range strikes escalate
links.excerpt: AP sets out the strike claim, the Samara-region fallout and the broader expansion of Ukraine's domestically built deep-strike campaign.
links.imageAlt: Ukraine says its drones hit another refinery deep inside Russia as long-range strikes escalate

links.headline: Ukrainian drones strike Russia's Syzran oil refinery, Kyiv says
links.excerpt: This Reuters carry adds refinery scale, timing and the claim that May has seen an unusually concentrated run of attacks on Russian oil plants.
links.imageAlt: Ukrainian drones strike Russia's Syzran oil refinery, Kyiv says

links.headline: Ukrainian deep-strike drones hit Russia's Syzran oil refinery
links.excerpt: Ukrainska Pravda emphasizes the military framing from Kyiv and the role of special-operations and drone-force units in the attack.
links.imageAlt: Ukrainian deep-strike drones hit Russia's Syzran oil refinery

stories.imageAlt: Ukraine says its drones hit another refinery deep inside Russia as long-range strikes escalate

stories.title: UN pressure builds on Hamas disarmament as Gaza's ceasefire framework looks increasingly brittle
stories.summary: The immediate diplomatic push is no longer just about aid flow or reconstruction sequencing but about whether any Gaza end-state can proceed without a disarmament deal. That makes the next round of UN and regional pressure more consequential than routine ceasefire maintenance.

links.headline: UN council urged to use 'every means at its disposal' to press Hamas to disarm
links.excerpt: AP captures the Security Council message that the ceasefire's survival now depends on moving the disarmament question.
links.imageAlt: UN council urged to use 'every means at its disposal' to press Hamas to disarm

links.headline: Board of Peace will ask the UN Security Council to press Hamas to disarm
links.excerpt: This earlier AP report adds the roadmap details linking weapons decommissioning to reconstruction, Israeli withdrawal and a technocratic Palestinian administration.
links.imageAlt: Board of Peace will ask the UN Security Council to press Hamas to disarm

links.headline: Israel threatens Gaza war resumption to force disarmament as 'truce' frays
links.excerpt: Al Jazeera highlights Palestinian objections to tying humanitarian relief and long-term governance to weapons surrender terms.
links.imageAlt: Israel threatens Gaza war resumption to force disarmament as 'truce' frays

stories.imageAlt: UN council urged to use 'every means at its disposal' to press Hamas to disarm

stories.title: Latvia scrambles NATO aircraft again as the Baltic drone spillover problem spreads
stories.summary: What looked like a one-off Baltic airspace scare has become a repeated NATO-border management problem. Latvia's alert suggests alliance capitals are now dealing with a pattern of ambiguous incursions that could force faster rules on interception, jamming and attribution.

links.headline: Latvia issues drone alert, scrambles NATO fighter jets
links.excerpt: Reuters provides the core facts of the latest alert and frames it as part of a widening chain of Baltic security incidents.

links.headline: NATO scrambles fighter jets after unidentified drone enters Latvian airspace
links.excerpt: BGNES adds the regional-security context and the sense that consecutive alerts are hardening Baltic concern.
links.imageAlt: NATO scrambles fighter jets after unidentified drone enters Latvian airspace

links.headline: Ukraine war latest: NATO jets scrambled again after drone enters Latvian airspace
links.excerpt: Sky News connects the alert to the broader Baltic trend and the live NATO debate over force posture in Europe.

stories.imageAlt: NATO scrambles fighter jets after unidentified drone enters Latvian airspace

stories.title: Capitol officers move to block Trump's new anti-weaponization payout fund from reaching Jan. 6 defendants
stories.summary: This has become a test of how far the administration is prepared to push grievance-based legal redress for its allies. The lawsuits and political backlash matter because the issue sits at the intersection of executive power, public money and January 6 accountability.

links.headline: Officers who defended Capitol from rioters sue to block payouts from $1.8B 'anti-weaponization' fund
links.excerpt: AP explains the officers' legal theory and why the fund is already being challenged as an unlawful slush mechanism.
links.imageAlt: Officers who defended Capitol from rioters sue to block payouts from $1.8B 'anti-weaponization' fund

links.headline: Trump's $1.776 billion 'weaponization' fund sparks outrage, but court challenges will be tough
links.excerpt: Reuters adds legal analysis on why opponents see a major abuse risk even if striking it down may be complicated.

links.headline: Police sue Trump as Jan. 6 rioters gloat over 'weaponization' fund
links.excerpt: The NPR-affiliated write-up stresses the reaction from defendants and why plaintiffs say the fund could incentivize further threats.
links.imageAlt: Police sue Trump as Jan. 6 rioters gloat over 'weaponization' fund

stories.imageAlt: Officers who defended Capitol from rioters sue to block payouts from $1.8B 'anti-weaponization' fund

stories.title: Europe keeps hardening migration policy around deportations, return hubs and rights interpretation
stories.summary: European governments are moving from rhetorical pressure to actual legal and political rewrites of how removals should work. Even before a final operational model is settled, the center of gravity has clearly shifted toward more permissive deportation policy.

links.headline: Dozens of European nations sign off on new interpretation of rights convention in migration cases
links.excerpt: AP explains the political declaration and why rights groups see it as a meaningful loosening of past constraints.
links.imageAlt: Dozens of European nations sign off on new interpretation of rights convention in migration cases

links.headline: EU talks on 'return hubs' migration reform go into second day
links.excerpt: The AFP carry focuses on the operational argument for offshore return hubs and the pressure to finalize new rules quickly.
links.quality: AFP report carried by Hindustan Times

stories.imageAlt: Dozens of European nations sign off on new interpretation of rights convention in migration cases

stories.title: Mexico and the EU move to tighten trade links as North America's next USMCA fight approaches
stories.summary: This is partly about diversification and partly about hedging against Washington's volatility under Trump-era trade politics. The overlap between Mexico-EU outreach and looming USMCA review talks shows how supply-chain strategy is now openly geopolitical.

links.headline: Mexico and the European Union tighten their alliance in the face of Trump-era risks
links.excerpt: EL PAIS frames the deal-making as a strategic insurance policy against US pressure on trade, security and migration.
links.imageAlt: Mexico and the European Union tighten their alliance in the face of Trump-era risks

links.headline: US, Mexico agree to begin talks on USMCA reforms, timing unclear
links.excerpt: Reuters adds the immediate North American timetable and the uncertainty around when the formal reform push will start.
links.imageAlt: US, Mexico agree to begin talks on USMCA reforms, timing unclear

links.headline: 2026 Trade Policy Agenda
links.excerpt: The trade agenda is the primary-source baseline showing Washington's stated intent to use the 2026 USMCA review aggressively.

stories.imageAlt: Mexico and the European Union tighten their alliance in the face of Trump-era risks

stories.title: Nvidia delivers another enormous quarter, but the AI market is now watching concentration and deployment pace as much as growth
stories.summary: The numbers remain exceptional, yet the story has shifted from whether demand is real to how long infrastructure spending can compound at this speed. Investor attention is increasingly on concentration risk, capital intensity and how much of the AI build-out turns into durable platform power.

links.headline: Nvidia Q1 results surpass Wall Street expectations thanks to massive AI chip demand
links.excerpt: AP distills the core earnings beat and the still-accelerating scale of Nvidia's data-center business.
links.imageAlt: Nvidia Q1 results surpass Wall Street expectations thanks to massive AI chip demand

links.headline: Nvidia posts another record quarter, reveals $43B of holdings in startups
links.excerpt: TechCrunch adds the unusually large venture-investment angle and why it matters for Nvidia's ecosystem strategy.
links.imageAlt: Nvidia posts another record quarter, reveals $43B of holdings in startups

links.headline: Nvidia posts record profit and revenue amid AI chip boom
links.excerpt: Al Jazeera emphasizes market reaction, shareholder returns and the question of whether results can keep outrunning expectations.
links.imageAlt: Nvidia posts record profit and revenue amid AI chip boom

stories.imageAlt: Nvidia Q1 results surpass Wall Street expectations thanks to massive AI chip demand

stories.title: AMD commits more than $10 billion to Taiwan's AI ecosystem as supply-chain competition with Nvidia intensifies
stories.summary: AMD is signaling that AI competition is no longer just about chip design but also about packaging, manufacturing depth and partner coordination. The Taiwan investment makes that supply-chain race explicit and gives Lisa Su another way to argue AMD can scale with hyperscaler demand.

links.headline: AMD says it will invest over $10 billion across Taiwan's AI ecosystem
links.excerpt: Reuters outlines the headline commitment and the core packaging and manufacturing partnerships AMD is trying to deepen.
links.quality: Reuters report carried by Yahoo Finance
links.imageAlt: AMD says it will invest over $10 billion across Taiwan's AI ecosystem

links.headline: AMD announces more than $10 billion in Taiwan ecosystem investments to accelerate AI infrastructure
links.excerpt: The Reuters brief adds the infrastructure language around Helios, Venice and the multi-gigawatt deployment push.

links.headline: AMD CEO Lisa Su arrives in Taipei
links.excerpt: Taiwan News gives local context on Su's visit and the broader Taiwan-side significance of AMD's ecosystem positioning.
links.imageAlt: AMD CEO Lisa Su arrives in Taipei

stories.imageAlt: AMD says it will invest over $10 billion across Taiwan's AI ecosystem

stories.title: Airbus warns customers that A350 delivery friction is still not fully under control
stories.summary: The fresh warning matters because widebody demand remains strong and airlines are already planning years ahead around constrained fleets. Even modest delivery slippage can cascade into route planning, cargo capacity and airline capital spending.

links.headline: Airbus warns some customers of further A350 delays, sources say
links.excerpt: Reuters focuses on supply-chain trouble around key fuselage and freighter components feeding the A350 line.
links.imageAlt: Airbus warns some customers of further A350 delays, sources say

links.headline: Airbus warns customers of fresh A350 delivery delays
links.excerpt: Air Data News spells out the customer impact and the way inherited Spirit-era disruptions are still complicating output.
links.imageAlt: Airbus warns customers of fresh A350 delivery delays

stories.imageAlt: Airbus warns some customers of further A350 delays, sources say

stories.title: Arafura's final investment decision turns Australia's rare-earth diversification push into an actual build
stories.summary: The Nolans decision matters beyond one mine because Western governments have spent years talking about non-China supply chains without many shovel-ready successes. This project gives Australia a more concrete role in the strategic minerals contest tied to defense, magnets and advanced manufacturing.

links.headline: Australia's Arafura approves $1.6 billion rare earths project
links.excerpt: Reuters frames Nolans as a strategically important addition to rare-earth capacity outside China.

links.headline: Strategic reserve supporting Arafura final investment decision
links.excerpt: The minister's release shows the government is explicitly tying the project to national and allied critical-minerals resilience.

links.headline: Arafura Rare Earths announces final investment decision to deliver the Nolans Project
links.excerpt: This Reuters-linked market item adds the company-facing framing as the project shifts from financing to execution.
links.quality: Reuters market report carried by TradingView
links.imageAlt: Arafura Rare Earths announces final investment decision to deliver the Nolans Project

stories.imageAlt: Arafura Rare Earths announces final investment decision to deliver the Nolans Project

stories.title: Australia's unemployment jump gives the budget and the RBA a harder growth-versus-inflation problem
stories.summary: The rise to 4.5% does not mean the labor market has broken, but it does suggest the economy is softening while inflation and the oil shock still threaten policy. That combination complicates both rate decisions and the government's post-budget sales pitch.

links.headline: Labour Force, Australia, April 2026
links.excerpt: The ABS release provides the primary data showing employment fell and the unemployment rate rose to 4.5% in April.
links.imageAlt: Labour Force, Australia, April 2026

links.headline: Australia's unemployment rate jumps to 4.5% in 'tentative signs labour market is buckling'
links.excerpt: The Guardian ties the data to rate expectations and the budget's warning that a worse oil shock could push unemployment higher.
links.imageAlt: Australia's unemployment rate jumps to 4.5% in 'tentative signs labour market is buckling'

links.headline: Drop in female employment drives jobless rate increase
links.excerpt: SBS emphasizes the composition of the labor-market deterioration, especially the notable fall in female employment.
links.imageAlt: Drop in female employment drives jobless rate increase

links.headline: Surprise unemployment jump eases interest rate fears
links.excerpt: The New Daily focuses on how markets quickly repriced the odds of another near-term hike after the release.

stories.title: Australia's diphtheria outbreak forces a larger federal health response as border-spanning case counts keep rising
stories.summary: The outbreak has moved beyond a localized remote-health story into a national test of vaccine delivery, public communication and Indigenous-community coordination. The fresh federal package shows Canberra now sees the scale as too large to leave to patchy state messaging alone.

links.headline: NT health authorities MIA as diphtheria spreads across Indigenous remote communities
links.excerpt: ABC reports the communication gap in the NT response as Australia's largest diphtheria outbreak on record keeps widening.
links.imageAlt: NT health authorities MIA as diphtheria spreads across Indigenous remote communities

links.outlet: Department of Health, Disability and Ageing
links.headline: $7.2 million response package to Diphtheria outbreak
links.excerpt: The federal release sets out the money for surge staff, vaccines, antibiotics and Aboriginal-community support work.

links.headline: Diphtheria outbreak update
links.excerpt: The Australian CDC provides the national status view and the formal outbreak-management guidance now being used by states and territories.

links.headline: New funding announced to fight deadly diphtheria outbreak in Australia
links.excerpt: SBS adds the community-health angle and the push for culturally appropriate communication in affected remote regions.
links.imageAlt: New funding announced to fight deadly diphtheria outbreak in Australia

stories.imageAlt: NT health authorities MIA as diphtheria spreads across Indigenous remote communities

stories.title: Labor's capital-gains changes keep drawing startup and small-business backlash even as the government holds the line
stories.summary: The argument is no longer confined to property tax politics; it has broadened into a live fight over startup incentives, entrepreneurial risk and budget strategy. Canberra is still defending the reform as fairness and housing policy, but the political cost is clearly rising.

links.headline: Labor walks tightrope as tax overhaul alarms startups and investors
links.excerpt: ABC lays out why founders, younger investors and some small businesses think the budget changes hit risk-taking too broadly.
links.imageAlt: Labor walks tightrope as tax overhaul alarms startups and investors

links.headline: Labor denies CGT tax reform will 'kill startups' as tech giant Canva warns of stifling innovation
links.excerpt: The Guardian highlights the business-sector campaign for exemptions and Labor's refusal to concede the broader point.
links.imageAlt: Labor denies CGT tax reform will 'kill startups' as tech giant Canva warns of stifling innovation

links.headline: Coalition positions itself as small business champion after Labor's budget tax reforms
links.excerpt: SBS shows how the opposition is trying to turn founder and small-business anger into a wider post-budget political attack.
links.imageAlt: Coalition positions itself as small business champion after Labor's budget tax reforms

links.headline: Should startup businesses be exempt from capital gains tax?
links.excerpt: This shorter ABC report captures the concentrated founder complaint that productive investment may be getting swept up with housing reform.
links.imageAlt: Should startup businesses be exempt from capital gains tax?

stories.imageAlt: Labor walks tightrope as tax overhaul alarms startups and investors

stories.title: Sara Duterte's impeachment case moves into the answer stage while the wider Senate crisis still hangs over the trial
stories.summary: The immediate development is procedural, but it matters because the trial is unfolding amid an already destabilized Senate and broader constitutional tension. The next question is whether the court can keep the process credible while factional conflict keeps spilling into the chamber's politics.

links.headline: Sara has until June 1 to answer impeach summons
links.excerpt: Philstar focuses on the new deadline and the formal service of summons to the vice president's office.
links.imageAlt: Sara has until June 1 to answer impeach summons

links.headline: Philippine Senate convenes as impeachment court to try vice president as political storm rages
links.excerpt: AP provides the outside-world framing on why the trial is becoming a broader stability test for Manila's institutions.
links.imageAlt: Philippine Senate convenes as impeachment court to try vice president as political storm rages

links.headline: House prosecution sees Senate 'chaos' tied to Duterte impeachment
links.excerpt: This earlier Philstar report shows how prosecution allies see the Senate upheaval as directly connected to the impeachment fight.
links.imageAlt: House prosecution sees Senate 'chaos' tied to Duterte impeachment

stories.imageAlt: Sara has until June 1 to answer impeach summons

stories.title: Chinese research-vessel activity around Pag-asa is sharpening another round of West Philippine Sea pressure
stories.summary: The significance here is not just a single vessel but the combination of survey work, coast-guard presence and personnel landings near a Philippine-held position. That mix keeps turning gray-zone activity into a direct sovereignty and security issue for Manila.

links.headline: Chinese from survey ship land on sandbars within Pag-asa waters
links.excerpt: Philstar details the reported landings near Cay-2 and Cay-3 and why Philippine officials treated them as a serious escalation.
links.imageAlt: Chinese from survey ship land on sandbars within Pag-asa waters

links.headline: PCG aircraft dispatched to drive away Chinese research vessel
links.excerpt: The follow-up Philstar report adds the Philippine Coast Guard's immediate response and the monitoring around the same vessel.

links.headline: DND, AFP reject China's claims, assert sovereignty over West Philippine Sea features
links.excerpt: ABS-CBN captures Manila's official pushback and the sovereignty framing that now surrounds the Pag-asa incidents.

stories.imageAlt: Chinese from survey ship land on sandbars within Pag-asa waters
